rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA METZGAR VS. BRIAN KENDALL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement (Ccp 664.6)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2025 line 10. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PER CCP SECTION 664.6: HEARING REQUIRED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CAMILA DE ANDRADE BIANCHI ET AL VS. VALERIE MCCARTHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is DENIED. There is no competent evidence of breach; Plaintiff's only evidence is counsel's conclusory statement that "Defendants did not make the June 1, 2025 payment or the July 1, 2025 payment[.]" (Ellson Decl. 10). Declarations filed on August 21, 2025 (40 minutes before the original hearing date) are stricken and not considered. No authority provided to support timeliness and procedural propriety of such filings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: QI MING WANG VS. WEIWEI ZHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS Hearing Required. Plaintiff's counsel and Claire Brett to appear in person or via Zoom with video capability. No later than 9:00 a.m. Plaintiff's counsel shall provide a receipt for FedEx overnight mailing with tracking number for the motion and supporting documents no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 22, 2025. The Court notes that the motion was submitted for filing at 10:47 a.m. on August 18, 2025; the proofs of services included with the motion papers attest to overnight mailing via FedEx on August 18, 2025; the proof of service is executed at Johannesburg, South Africa. Furthermore, counsel for Plaintiff no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 22, 2025 shall either (1) provide a copy of a signed and filed endorsed copy of the purported June 10, 2025 "Order" referenced in paragraph 8 of Sikavi Declaration or (2) provide a brief limited to citations to authorities (no argument), not to exceed 2 pages, addressing the inclusion of what appears to be a print-out from the Court's tentative ruling web-page characterizing it as a true and correct copy of an "Order" of the Court. If no such authorities found, brief shall be filed and state "none." Additionally, a filed endorsed courtesy copy of a Request for Judicial Notice referenced in the Points and Authorities shall be delivered to the Court no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 22, 2025. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MM40LOOM LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. OLIVE CHENG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2025 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF DEFENDANTS' ANSWERS is DENIED without prejudice. This motion seeks to strike the entirety of 3 answers on file (except for the first two pages) on the grounds that "the answers contain irrelevant and improper matter, including voluminous unnecessary attachments containing, among other things, confidential bank account number and settlement correspondence." The only "specifics" as to the matter subject to being stricken included in the Notice and motion are "bank account number" and "settlement correspondence." There is no indication that the entirety of all the attachments are bank account numbers and settlement correspondence. There is no discussion as to why each page of the answer is subject to being struck. The Court notes that Plaintiff filed an untimely supplemental brief, which is stricken. However, the Court notes that the case cited in the untimely brief exemplifies the need to address and analyze each and every document allegedly improperly filed. See Overstock.com, Inc. v. Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 471, 508-510. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
